input.prompt:
I have a .NET 10 CoreCLR web API running on Linux. I need to configure
automatic crash dump collection so that when the app crashes, a full
dump is written to /var/dumps. Show me the environment variables to set
and how to verify the configuration.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET 10 CoreCLR web API running on Linux. I need to configure
automatic crash dump collection so that when the app crashes, a full
dump is written to /var/dumps. Show me the environment variables to set
and how to verify the configuration.

data.content:
Here's how to configure automatic crash dump collection for .NET on Linux:

### Environment Variables

```bash
# Enable crash dumps and set the output path
export DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4          # 4 = Full dump (heap + stack + modules)
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/var/dumps/coredump.%p.%t
```

**Dump type values:**
| Value | Type |
|-------|------|
| 1 | Normal (stack only) |
| 2 | WithHeap |
| 3 | Triage |
| 4 | Full |

`%p` = PID, `%t` = timestamp — prevents overwriting on repeated crashes.

### Setup & Verify

```bash
# Ensure the dump directory exists and is writable
sudo mkdir -p /var/dumps
sudo chmod 1777 /var/dumps

# Verify the variables are set
env | grep DOTNET_Dbg

# (Optional) Disable the OS core dump to avoid duplicates
ulimit -c 0
```

### Test It

```bash
# Send SIGABRT to force a crash and confirm a dump is written
kill -6 <PID_OF_YOUR_APP>

# Check for the dump file
ls -lh /var/dumps/
```

### For systemd Services

Add the variables to your unit file:

```ini
[Service]
Environment=DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
Environment=DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4
Environment=DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/var/dumps/coredump.%p.%t
```

Then `sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl restart your-app`.

> **Note:** The older `COMPlus_Dbg*` prefix still works but `DOTNET_Dbg*` is the canonical form since .NET 6+.
